message:
test(scan_pipeline): pin per-category trigger count parity across chunk boundaries (ft-v5do5)

## Coverage gap

proptest_scan_pipeline.rs had three chunked-vs-batch trigger parity
properties, all of which assert only `batch_total == chunked_total`
via `triggers.total_matches`. None compared the per-category
distribution. A future refactor that miscounts one category and
compensates in another — e.g. an off-by-one in the overlap-skip
check at `scan_chunk_triggers_with_overlap` that drops an Error
match while spuriously counting a Warning — would pass all three
tests while quietly corrupting the per-category analytics the
rate-limit detector, error clustering, and workflow router depend on.

## Fix

Two new proptests inline with the existing block:

  - chunked_batch_per_category_trigger_parity — 256 cases of
    arbitrary chunk boundaries, compression OFF. Asserts
    `batch.counts == chunked.counts` (HashMap equality), strictly
    stronger than the existing total_matches equality.

  - chunked_batch_per_category_trigger_parity_with_compression —
    same assertion with compression_threshold=1 so the chunked path
    routes through `uncompressed_buffer` for the definitive flush
    scan (a different code path than the no-compression branch
    that uses `trigger_data_buffer`).

## Verification

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p1-target cargo test -p frankenterm-core \
    --test proptest_scan_pipeline --no-default-features \
    --features asupersync-runtime chunked_batch_per_category
  → running 2 tests; 2 passed, 0 failed.

No production code changed — this is test-only strengthening that
pins an invariant the current implementation already satisfies
(flush uses the same `scan_counts` call as batch on identical bytes)
but which nothing pinned until now.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
